title: Best Smart / Microchip Pet Door: Let Only Your Pet In
meta: Microchip and collar-tag smart pet doors compared on compatibility, security and installation.
intro: A smart pet door ends the 'let the dog out at 6am' routine and keeps raccoons out. The key is matching the door to your pet's microchip or tag.

sections.body: Microchip-reading doors never get 'lost' like a collar tag can, but only work if your pet is chipped. Collar-tag doors are cheaper and work for any pet but fail if the collar comes off.
A smart door that reads your pet's chip won't open for the neighbor's cat or a raccoon. Check the locking modes (in-only, out-only, full, locked).
Most mount in a standard door or a wall. Wall installs need a tunnel kit. Measure your pet's shoulder height — mount the flap so they don't trip.

products.name: Microchip-Reading Smart Pet Door
products.blurb: Reads your pet's existing chip; no collar needed.

products.name: Smart Pet Door Wall Installation Kit

faq.q: Will it work with any microchip?
faq.a: Most read the common 9, 10 and 15-digit ISO chips, but confirm your pet's chip type against the door spec.

faq.q: Can I keep my pet in at night?
faq.a: Yes — all smart doors have a manual lock mode for nighttime or weather.
